module.name: type-guards
module.description: Runtime type-guard functions shared across rule implementations and AST helpers.

scenarios.name: should return true for boolean true
scenarios.then: returns true and narrows the value to boolean

scenarios.name: should return true for boolean false
scenarios.then: returns true and narrows the value to boolean

scenarios.name: should return false for a string
scenarios.then: returns false because the value is not a boolean primitive

scenarios.name: should return false for a number
scenarios.then: returns false because the value is not a boolean primitive

scenarios.name: should return true for a non-null, non-undefined value
scenarios.then: returns true and narrows the input to a defined value

scenarios.name: should return false for null
scenarios.then: returns false because null is excluded from the defined branch

scenarios.name: should return false for undefined
scenarios.then: returns false because undefined is excluded from the defined branch

scenarios.name: should return true for null
scenarios.then: returns true and narrows the value to null | undefined

scenarios.name: should return true for undefined
scenarios.then: returns true and narrows the value to null | undefined

scenarios.name: should return false for a defined value
scenarios.then: returns false because the value is neither null nor undefined

scenarios.name: should return true for a numeric value
scenarios.then: returns true and narrows the value to number

scenarios.name: should return true for NaN (runtime typeof is 'number')
scenarios.then: returns true because typeof NaN is 'number'

scenarios.name: should return false for a string that looks like a number
scenarios.then: returns false because string input does not satisfy the numeric guard

scenarios.name: should return true for an object literal
scenarios.given: a standard object literal with Object.prototype
scenarios.then: returns true and safely narrows the value to Record<string, unknown>

scenarios.name: should return true for an object with a null prototype
scenarios.then: returns true and safely narrows the value to Record<string, unknown>

scenarios.name: should return false for arrays and null
scenarios.then: returns false because those values are not plain object records

scenarios.name: should return false for Date, Map, and class instances
scenarios.given: built-in or custom objects whose prototype is not Object.prototype or null
scenarios.then: returns false so non-plain objects are not misclassified as records

scenarios.name: should return true for a string value
scenarios.then: returns true and narrows the value to string

scenarios.name: should return true for an empty string
scenarios.then: returns true and narrows the value to string

scenarios.name: should return false for a number
scenarios.then: returns false because numeric input does not satisfy the string guard

scenarios.name: should return false for null
scenarios.then: returns false because null is not a string
